questions.question: Define a cyclic group with an example. Is Z8 under addition modulo 8 a cyclic group?
keyPoints: Definition: A group G is cyclic if there exists an element g ∈ G such that every element of G can be expressed as g^n for some integer n
Example: Z6 = {0,1,2,3,4,5} under addition modulo 6
Yes, Z8 is cyclic because 1 generates all elements: ⟨1⟩ = {0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7}

questions.question: State and prove that every cyclic group is abelian.
keyPoints: Statement: All cyclic groups are abelian
Proof: Let G = ⟨g⟩ be cyclic. For any a = g^m, b = g^n in G: a·b = g^m · g^n = g^(m+n) = g^n · g^m = b·a
Since the operation is commutative for powers of g, the group is abelian.

questions.question: Define the order of an element and the order of a group. Find the order of element 2 in Z10.
keyPoints: Order of group: Number of elements in the group
Order of element a: Smallest positive n such that a^n = e
In Z10, 2 has order 5 because 2×5 = 10 ≡ 0 (mod 10) and no smaller positive integer works

questions.question: What is the Klein four-group V4? Show that it is isomorphic to Z2 × Z2.
keyPoints: V4 = {e, a, b, ab} with a²=b²=(ab)²=e, all elements of order 2
Z2 × Z2 = {(0,0),(1,0),(0,1),(1,1)} with component-wise addition
This is a bijective homomorphism, hence an isomorphism.

questions.question: Define the dihedral group D4. List its elements and their types (rotation or reflection).
keyPoints: D4: Symmetries of a square (regular 4-gon), order 8
Rotations: r0 (identity), r90, r180, r270
Reflections: s (vertical), sr, sr², sr³
r^k represents rotation by k×90°, s is reflection along vertical axis

questions.question: What is the unit group U(n)? Find U(12) and its order.
keyPoints: U(n) = {a ∈ {1,2,...,n-1} : gcd(a,n) = 1} under multiplication modulo n
U(12) = {1, 5, 7, 11} (elements relatively prime to 12)

questions.question: Define the Quaternion group Q8. Show that every element except -1 has order 4 in Q8.
keyPoints: Q8 = {±1, ±i, ±j, ±k} with multiplication rules: i²=j²=k²=ijk=-1
Order of 1: 1, Order of -1: 2 (since (-1)² = 1)
For i: i² = -1, i³ = -i, i⁴ = 1, so order is 4
Similarly, -i, j, -j, k, -k all have order 4

questions.question: Define: (i) Cyclic group (ii) Symmetric group Sn (iii) Alternating group An (iv) Dihedral group Dn. For n=3, find the orders of S3 and A3.
keyPoints: (i) Cyclic: Generated by single element, all elements are powers of generator
(ii) Sn: All permutations of {1,2,...,n}, order n!
(iii) An: Even permutations of n elements, order n!/2
(iv) Dn: Symmetries of regular n-gon, order 2n
A3 = {e, (123), (132)} is cyclic of order 3

questions.question: Show that Zp (where p is prime) under addition modulo p is a cyclic group. Find all generators of Z7.
keyPoints: Z_p is cyclic because 1 generates all elements: ⟨1⟩ = {0,1,2,...,p-1}
Also any element a where gcd(a,p)=1 generates the group
For p=7 (prime), all non-zero elements are generators: 1,2,3,4,5,6

questions.question: Define subgroups of Zn under addition. Show that ⟨d⟩ is a subgroup of Zn where d divides n. What is the order of this subgroup?
keyPoints: In Zn under addition, for any divisor d of n, ⟨d⟩ = {0, d, 2d, ..., (n/d-1)d} is a subgroup
This is the cyclic subgroup generated by d
Number of elements in ⟨d⟩ = n/d (the smallest k such that kd ≡ 0 mod n)
Since d|n, n = dk for some k, and kd = n ≡ 0, so order is k = n/d
Example: In Z12, ⟨4⟩ = {0,4,8}, order 3 = 12/4

questions.question: Find all proper subgroups of S3. Show that S3 is not cyclic but A3 is cyclic.
keyPoints: S3 has order 6. Proper subgroups: ⟨e⟩ = {e} (order 1), ⟨(12)⟩, ⟨(13)⟩, ⟨(23)⟩ (each order 2), ⟨(123)⟩ = {e, (123), (132)} (order 3)
S3 is not cyclic because it is non-abelian (no single generator can produce all 6 elements)
A3 = {e, (123), (132)} has 3 elements, is abelian, and (123) generates it
Thus A3 ≅ Z3 is cyclic, but S3 is not
